title: Borrelia crocidurae meningoencephalitis in a young Senegalense man returning to Tenerife, Canary Islands.

sections.title: Borrelia crocidurae meningoencephalitis in a young Senegalense man returning to Tenerife, Canary Islands.
sections.text: Borrelia crocidurae infection is considered endemic in West Africa. We describe a B. crocidurae meningoencephalitis in a young Senegalese man returning to the Canary Islands from his home country. Clinical suspicion in patients from endemic areas and molecular diagnostic techniques are essential for accurate identification of this pathogen.
